title: Handle locale and i18n in preparation
description: Locale, language, and formatting are explicit inputs to assembly so the same template serves multiple locales correctly.
body: Use when the system serves more than one language or region. Do not hardcode English phrasing, date/number formats, or a single locale into the template, and avoid assuming the input language equals the output language. Actions: take locale as an explicit parameter, select localized template fragments, format dates/numbers per locale, and specify the desired output language in the prompt. Validation signals include a test that switching locale yields correctly localized formatting and language without altering the instruction structure. Risk boundary: a hardcoded locale silently mis-serves other regions; treat locale as a first-class input, not an afterthought.
